prompt_text: Compare Neon and Supabase for spin up serverless Postgres databases instantly with auto-suspend
response:
# Neon vs Supabase: Serverless Postgres Comparison

## Auto-Suspend Feature

| Feature | Neon | Supabase |
|---------|------|---------|
| **Auto-suspend** | ✅ Yes (default feature) | ❌ Not available |
| **Suspend idle time** | Configurable (5-600 seconds) | N/A |
| **Cold start** | ~1 second | Not applicable |
| **Cost benefit** | High (don't pay for idle time) | Lower (always-on billing) |

## Quick Spinup

| Aspect | Neon | Supabase |
|--------|------|---------|
| **Spinup time** | ~30 seconds | ~1-2 minutes |
| **API ready** | Fast | Moderate |
| **Dashboard UX** | Excellent | Good |

## Pricing Model

```
NEON:
- Pro: $19/month
- Includes auto-suspend
- Pay per compute hour used
- Better for variable workloads

SUPABASE:
- Pro: $25/month
- Always-on compute
- Fixed monthly cost
- Better for consistent usage
```

## Key Differences

### Neon Advantages
- 🎯 **Auto-suspend = cost savings** (best for dev/test)
- Faster cold starts (~1s vs variable)
- Pay-per-use model
- PostgreSQL optimized

### Supabase Advantages
- 🔌 **Richer ecosystem** (Auth, Storage, Realtime built-in)
- No cold starts
- More managed features out-of-box
- Better for production with predictable traffic

## When to Choose

**Choose Neon if:**
- Budget-conscious development
- Sporadic/bursty workloads
- Testing & staging environments
- Minimal auth/storage needs

**Choose Supabase if:**
- Need integrated Auth/Storage/Realtime
- Predictable, consistent traffic
- Production workloads
- Want all-in-one platform

## Verdict

For **auto-suspend specifically**: **Neon wins decisively** — it's the primary differentiator. For **complete serverless platform**: **Supabase** offers more features, though without cost-saving auto-suspend.
